invented_voltage_level.evidence: Response states 'no voltage value is printed on this sheet, so the drawing does not establish the DI voltage' and hedges the 24VDC mention as 'typically...but confirm' — matches the image, which shows no voltage callouts anywhere in the P02.00 table.

invented_device_tag.evidence: Only device tag used is 'GS20(X)', which is visibly printed in the black label box in all three diagram rows of the image.

incorrect_terminal_or_destination_as_fac.evidence: Terminal names FWD/DI1, REV/DI2, DI3, DCM used in the response all appear verbatim in the image (e.g., 'FWD/DI1', 'REV/DI2', 'DI3', 'DCM' labels in each row), and the open/close-to-function mapping (e.g., Setting 2: FWD/DI1 'CLOSE': RUN, REV/DI2 'CLOSE': REV) matches the printed table text.

missed_hazardous_voltage_clearly_shown.evidence: The image contains only low-voltage control-terminal diagrams (switch contacts to DI1/DI2/DI3/DCM); no power, motor, or bus-voltage conductors are drawn on this page, so there is no clearly-shown hazardous voltage to miss.

false_certainty_on_illegible_area.evidence: The response explicitly flags the Setting-3 junction (STOP/RUN/DI3) as small and needing a higher-resolution crop rather than asserting it with full certainty, consistent with the genuinely cramped symbol cluster in that row of the image.

non_electrical_image_treated_as_print.evidence: The image is unambiguously an electrical control-wiring table/diagram (P02.00 settings with FWD/DI1, REV/DI2, DCM terminals), and the response correctly treats it as such.

sheet_identity.note: Correctly ties response to 'Page 2 of an Automation Direct application note,' matching the footer 'Page 2 of 3' in the image; does not claim it is the reversing-contactor/braking sheet implied by the source metadata, which is appropriate since that content is not visible here.

circuit_purpose.note: Accurately summarizes the page's purpose: setting P00.21 and P02.00 to define 2-wire/3-wire FWD-STOP/REV control from digital inputs, matching the bullet text and table headings 'External Terminal Control Circuits'.

device_identification.note: Correctly lists the single device as the GS20(X) drive control-terminal block, matching the 'GS20(X)' label visible in each diagram row.

wire_cable_identification.note: Labels like 'FWD/STOP -> FWD/DI1' and 'STOP+RUN -> FWD/DI1' reasonably describe the switch-to-terminal connections shown, but the diagram uses contact symbols, not labeled wire numbers, so calling these 'control wire' entries adds a layer of interpretation beyond what is printed.

terminal_mapping.note: FWD/DI1, REV/DI2, DI3, and DCM mappings correctly mirror the terminal labels printed in the three rows of the P02.00 table.

voltage_identification.note: Correctly states 'no voltage value is printed on this sheet' — accurate, since the image shows no voltage ratings anywhere in the table or diagrams.

cross_reference_accuracy.note: Cites 'Control Circuit Wiring Diagrams... Chapter 2... Source Mode' which matches the printed note: 'See the “Control Circuit Wiring Diagrams” in the table of contents in chapter 2 of the user manual for “Source Mode” wiring examples.'

uncertainty_calibration.note: Flags the cramped Setting-3 junction and the unlabeled DI voltage as unverified, matching genuinely ambiguous/absent details in the image; confidence numbers (0.85-0.97) are somewhat arbitrary but directionally consistent with which items are less certain.

unsupported_or_invented_claims.note: The map follow-up includes the stray fragment '🧩 1 off-page ref(s): GS20/GS20X User Manual — Control Circuit Wiring Diagrams (Ch.2) · 3 PLC I/O' — the '3 PLC I/O' clause has no basis in the image and is not explained, an unsupported/garbled insertion.

technician_usefulness.note: Provides parameter settings, terminal roles, and NPN/PNP wiring guidance that a technician could act on directly, matching the bullet-list and diagram content of the image.

safety_language.note: Correctly instructs to de-energize/lockout/verify absence of voltage before continuity checks and separately cautions about lethal power/motor terminals not shown on this page, which is reasonable general practice given no power circuitry appears in this image.

readability.note: Heavy emoji-sectioning and dense bullet nesting (e.g., '🔌 Signals', '🔧 Devices', '🩺 If you're chasing a fault') reduce quick scannability for a print review despite being organized.

response_completeness.note: Covers all three P02.00 settings, the P00.21 enable bit, and the NPN/PNP DCM note, matching all textual and tabular content visible on the page.

map_offer_supported.note: The follow-up 'map' reply delivers signal/terminal/wire detail consistent with what was promised, though it introduces the unexplained '3 PLC I/O' artifact noted above.

verified_strengths: Correctly reads P02.00 Setting 1/2/3 open-close logic exactly as printed (e.g., Setting 2: FWD/DI1 'CLOSE': RUN, REV/DI2 'CLOSE': REV).
Accurately reflects the printed NPN/PNP note about wiring back to DCM vs. supplying 24VDC and tying 0V to DCM.
Does not invent a voltage value; explicitly states none is printed on the sheet.
Uses only the device tag 'GS20(X)' that is actually printed in the diagram boxes.

suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.claim: 🧩 1 off-page ref(s): GS20/GS20X User Manual — Control Circuit Wiring Diagrams (Ch.2) · 3 PLC I/O
suspected_errors_or_hallucinations.why: The trailing '3 PLC I/O' fragment has no corresponding text, symbol, or reference in the image and is not explained; appears to be an unsupported/garbled artifact.

items_requiring_technician_review: Exact contact arrangement of STOP (NC) and RUN (NO) buttons relative to DI3 in the Setting-3 (three-wire) diagram — the junction is small in the source image.
Actual DI/DCM voltage level should be confirmed from the GS20/GS20X manual before any live measurement, since it is not printed on this page.
summary: The response accurately paraphrases the visible P02.00 table (Settings 1-3), the P00.21 enable bit, and the NPN/PNP DCM wiring note without inventing voltages, tags, or terminal destinations, and it appropriately hedges the one genuinely cramped diagram area. It loses points for an unsupported, unexplained '3 PLC I/O' fragment in the off-page-reference line and for dense, emoji-heavy formatting that hurts scannability, but no hard-failure-level fabrication is present.
